artifacts.summary: Official discovery identified one university-wide URK page on AI in education and one center-level ASC academic-integrity page with directly usable AI wording. WNE AI ethics pages timed out during fetch attempts, and a DELab/IDUB conference result was treated as event or advisory content rather than binding policy.

artifacts.claimText: University of Warsaw URK guidance says AI-system use in diploma work requires agreement between the supervisor and student, including scope and methods of use; written exams may not use AI systems unless exam instructions specify how they may be used.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Wytyczne dotyczące korzystania z narzędzi sztucznej inteligencji w procesie kształcenia
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: W przypadku prac dyplomowych podstawową regułę stanowi zasada, zgodnie z którą wykorzystanie systemów SI w przygotowaniu pracy wymaga uzgodnienia między opiekunem pracy dyplomowej i studentem... Ponadto za niedopuszczalne zostało uznane korzystanie z systemów SI podczas egzaminów pisemnych, chyba że polecenia egzaminacyjne lub treści zadań egzaminacyjnych określają sposób korzystania z systemów SI.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: For diploma work, AI-system use requires agreement between the supervisor and student; use of AI systems in written exams is considered impermissible unless the exam instructions specify how AI may be used.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Paragraphs beginning 'W przypadku prac dyplomowych' and 'W przypadku prac i prezentacji zaliczeniowych'
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For University of Warsaw American Studies Center students, ASC academic-integrity guidance says using generative AI tools to generate content and submitting it as one's own work is prohibited, and students should follow the course syllabus or ask instructors about permitted use.

citation.publisher: American Studies Center, University of Warsaw
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: It is important to remember that using GAITs to generate content and submitting it as your own work is prohibited. Your instructors will offer more specific guidelines on the appropriate use of such tools... ASC students should always consult the course syllabus for information about the scope and limitations of GAITs in a given course.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: ASC tells students that generating content with GAITs and submitting it as their own work is prohibited, and that course syllabi and instructors govern permitted use.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Sections 'Unfair Advantage' and 'The Scope of and Rules for Using Generative AI Tools'
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.publisher: American Studies Center, University of Warsaw
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow claim because the official URK page directly states the diploma-work agreement requirement and written-exam AI-use restriction. The claim does not assert human review or canonical publication.
Approved as guidance/recommendation only; wording avoids treating the recommendation as a binding requirement.
Approved only with explicit American Studies Center scope; the source is not treated as a university-wide rule.

artifacts.title: University of Warsaw AI Policy Staged Review
artifacts.summary: Staged review found official University of Warsaw URK guidance on AI tools in education, including diploma-work agreement/marking expectations, written-exam restrictions unless instructions allow AI use, and teaching recommendations. It also found an ASC-scoped academic-integrity page addressing generative AI use by ASC students.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The ASC academic-integrity claim is scoped to the American Studies Center and should not be generalized as a university-wide policy.
The WNE AI ethics source timed out during this run and should be retried before WNE-scoped research guidance is assessed.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
